description: Fix 6 CRITICAL issues identified during comprehensive billing audit: duplicated status mapping, missing paused status handling, unprotected lot edit page, missing building edit subscription check, fail-open security issue, and canAddProperty batch bypass.

userStories.title: Consolidate mapStripeStatus to single source of truth
userStories.description: As the system, I need a single mapStripeStatus function used everywhere, so that status mapping logic can't drift between files.
acceptanceCriteria: SubscriptionService.mapStripeStatus() is the single source (already exists, public static)
stripe-webhook.handler.ts imports and uses SubscriptionService.mapStripeStatus() instead of private duplicate
subscription-actions.ts verifyCheckoutSession uses SubscriptionService.mapStripeStatus() instead of inline map
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Removed private mapStripeStatus from webhook handler, replaced inline map in subscription-actions with static method.

userStories.title: Handle paused subscription status in checkReadOnly and checkCanAddProperty
userStories.description: As the system, when a subscription is paused, users should be in read-only mode and unable to add properties, so that billing enforcement is correct for all Stripe states.
acceptanceCriteria: checkReadOnly returns true when status is 'paused'
checkCanAddProperty returns false when status is 'paused'
Existing unit tests still pass
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Added paused to 3 places: checkReadOnly, checkCanAddProperty (private), canAddProperty (public).

userStories.title: Add subscription gate to lot edit page
userStories.description: As the system, I need to block editing of locked lots via direct URL navigation to /lots/modifier/[id], so that lot access restrictions can't be bypassed.
acceptanceCriteria: Lot edit page checks subscription and redirects to /gestionnaire/biens if lot is locked
Pattern matches existing lot detail page gate (try/catch with isLotLocked flag, redirect outside catch)
Fail-open on subscription check error (consistent with current pattern)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as lots/[id]/page.tsx. Imports subscription service + repository, checks before loading lot.

userStories.title: Add subscription check to updateCompleteProperty + canAddProperty(count)
userStories.description: As the system, I need updateCompleteProperty to verify subscription limits before adding new lots, and canAddProperty should check if N lots can be added (not just 1), to prevent batch bypass.
acceptanceCriteria: updateCompleteProperty checks subscription before adding new lots (data.lots.new.length > 0)
createCompleteProperty checks if team can add data.lots.length lots (not just 'can add 1')
canAddProperty accepts optional count parameter (default 1)
Blocked if actual_lots + count > subscribed_lots (for active/past_due/paused/incomplete)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: canAddProperty(teamId, count=1), createCompleteProperty passes lots.length, updateCompleteProperty adds full gate after building verification.

userStories.title: Change getAccessibleLotIds fail-open to fail-closed
userStories.description: As the system, when the lot query fails in getAccessibleLotIds, I should return an empty array (no access) instead of null (all access), so that a database error can't grant unauthorized access.
acceptanceCriteria: Query error returns empty array [] (no lots accessible) instead of null (all accessible)
Warning log is changed to error log with clear message
Callers handle empty array correctly (biens-page-client, building-details-client, lot detail page, intervention-actions)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Changed return null to return []. Verified all callers handle [] correctly (truthy in JS, empty .includes() returns false = lot locked).

userStories.title: Add error boundary + useSubscription error state for billing UI resilience
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, if subscription fetching fails, I should see a clear error message instead of a blank/crashed page, so that I understand what's happening.
acceptanceCriteria: useSubscription hook returns hasError boolean + error string
Settings page billing section wrapped in error boundary with fallback
Billing page shows error alert when subscription fetch fails
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Added hasError to UseSubscriptionReturn, BillingErrorBoundary class component wrapping SubscriptionSummaryCard in settings page.
